response.justification: Bogdan brings 18+ years of solid software engineering experience with TypeScript and Node.js, and his current role at re:cinq does involve MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure — directly relevant technology. However, the role requires 8+ years specifically building production-grade AI solutions, and his substantive AI work is confined to a single position started in April 2025. Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, yet is a primary required language. There is no evidence of vector database usage, RAG architecture design, LLM performance optimization, or multi-agent framework experience (LangGraph, CrewAI). The experience gap between general software engineering depth and the Staff-level GenAI expertise this role demands is too wide to overlook.
strengths: Direct hands-on work with Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, aligning with a key technology explicitly listed in the job description
Proven Staff-level engineering leadership across multiple organizations, with a demonstrated track record of mentoring, technical workshops, and driving engineering excellence

Extensive experience building high-throughput distributed systems and cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK at Optilyz, millions of req/s data integration at Dunnhumby), relevant to the integration and pipeline responsibilities of the role
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume skills section and all role descriptions, yet it is listed as a primary required language alongside TypeScript
No evidence of vector database experience, RAG architecture design, or LLM optimization — these are core technical requirements for the role, not merely preferred qualifications

The 8+ years of AI-solution experience required is not met; all identifiable AI and agentic work is limited to the current position started April 2025, representing roughly one month at time of writing
